scenario.title: Three code paths
scenario.question: Where should tag filtering and URL-state logic live, and which way should the DOM depend on it?
unresolved: control style, density, and empty-state copy belong to interface-directions
constraints: Node-only test runner (no DOM in tests)

options.label: Functional core, thin shell
options.claim: Keep all logic as plain importable functions; DOM wiring is a few lines of glue.
cons: DOM glue has no direct test coverage
each future widget wires itself by hand

seams.test: node --test imports both pure functions directly
seams.migration: purely additive; static no-JS list already complete

failureModes: manual DOM-sync mistake in the untested glue layer
changeConditions: a second widget must react to the same tag state
back/forward live-update becomes a hard requirement

options.label: Encapsulated custom element
options.claim: The catalog becomes a self-contained <skill-catalog> element owning state and rendering.
cons: HTMLElement does not exist in plain Node
needs a hand-maintained static fallback inside the tag

seams.test: requires a DOM shim, or extracting Path A's functions anyway

failureModes: attribute-change timing bugs invisible to the Node-only runner
changeConditions: the catalog must ship as a widget to other pages or projects

options.label: Pub/sub URL-state store
options.claim: A small store owns the URL and notifies any number of subscribers on every change.
cons: pub/sub machinery a single consumer does not need

seams.test: node --test passes a plain object as win
seams.migration: additive; extra bootstrap wiring at startup

changeConditions: back/forward correctness becomes an explicit requirement
a second independent UI piece must react to tag changes

recommendation.reason: The only shape with no tension against the Node-only test constraint: logic stays in plain functions testable exactly like visibleSkills(), with no DOM shim and no new dependency. B collapses into a wrapper around A once logic is extracted for testing; C answers requirements nobody has stated.

accepted.recordTitle: Code-path decision: tag filtering and URL-state structure
accepted.summary: Path A (functional core, thin shell) was selected and explicitly accepted in chat. Paths B and C remain recorded as rejected alternatives with their own change conditions. No production code has been modified.
accepted.acceptedVia: explicit chat confirmation after candidate export

decisions.rationale: Only shape with no tension against the Node-only test constraint; matches the existing pure-function idiom.
decisions.acceptedVia: chat: "accept path A" after reviewing the candidate export

exampleWorking.rationale: Only shape with no tension against the Node-only test constraint; matches the existing pure-function idiom.

accepted.canonicalBody: DecisionGate D-01 resolved: Path A — functional core, thin imperative shell. Paths B (custom element) and C (pub/sub store) preserved as rejected alternatives with change conditions. See the comparison matrix in the instrument evidence; interface control decisions remain with interface-directions.
